summary: Tests provide solid coverage of core validation (expired/warning/ok classification, day calculations, summary counts) and formatting (JSON and Markdown output) modules. Well-organized with describe blocks and very clear test names. Good boundary condition testing (14-day warning boundary, zero days to expiry, zero warning window). Good error scenario coverage (invalid dates, empty names, non-positive policies, invalid reference dates, missing secrets array). Notable gaps: (1) CLI/main.ts module entirely untested—no tests for argument parsing, file I/O, or JSON config errors, (2) missing code comments explaining testing approach despite explicit requirement, (3) some unusual edge cases not covered (future-dated secrets, very large policy days, whitespace-only names beyond just empty string). Integration tests in run-tests.ts provide end-to-end validation but don't replace unit coverage for main.ts. Would catch most regressions in validator/formatter logic but miss CLI layer.
